name: web_scraper
description: Scrape and extract structured data from public web pages. Get text, links, tables, or specific elements. Blocks internal/private network access.

action.description: What to extract: 'text' (full page text), 'links' (all links), 'tables' (HTML tables as JSON), 'elements' (CSS selector), 'structured' (auto-detect job postings, products, articles)

extract_fields.description: For 'elements' action: map of field_name → CSS selector relative to each element. E.g. {"title": "a", "location": ".location"}

instructions:
Use this tool to scrape data from web pages. Supports multiple extraction modes:

**Actions:**
- `text` — Get all visible text from the page
- `links` — Get all links with text and URLs
- `tables` — Extract HTML tables as JSON arrays
- `elements` — Extract specific elements using CSS selectors
- `structured` — Auto-detect structured data (job postings, products, articles)

**For job postings**, use action='elements' with a CSS selector for each job row, and extract_fields to map title, location, date, URL.

**Example:** Scrape job postings from a careers page:
```json
{"url": "https://example.com/careers", "action": "elements", "selector": ".job-row", "extract_fields": {"title": "a", "location": ".location"}}
```
